_comment: Drone photogrammetry ingest — RGB-coloured point cloud from a
Structure-from-Motion / Multi-View Stereo pipeline (Pix4D,
RealityCapture, Metashape, OpenDroneMap) into a MobilityDB
tpcpatch column.
Prerequisites:
1. Register pcid 700 (X/Y/Z + R/G/B). See examples/
drone_photogrammetry_ingest.sql.
2. The destination table drone_photo_surveys is created by the
same SQL file.
Source format:
This pipeline targets a .laz or .copc file. For .ply (the most
common photogrammetry export), swap readers.las for readers.ply
and drop the filters.assign for time_t (PLY has no per-point
time). For PLY without per-point timestamps, set time_t to the
campaign capture time so every point lands in a single instant:
filters.assign value=['time_t = 1714386000000000']
Why one tpcpatch row per survey:
Photogrammetry surveys are discrete campaigns (one flight, one
reconstruction). Storing each survey as one tpcpatch lets you
compare campaigns at the same site over time:
- pcpatch_icp(s1.cloud, s2.cloud, ...) on per-instant patches
gives the rigid alignment between two surveys (heritage
facade settlement, road surface change).
- tnpoints(cloud) over a campaign tells you reconstructed
density per timestamp, useful for QC.
- atGeometry(cloud, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON(...)')) clips a
survey to a parcel or bridge footprint.
